data.title: Iranian Parliament Deputy Speaker: First revenue from Strait of Hormuz toll fees has been deposited.
data.description: The Deputy Speaker of the Iranian Parliament stated that the first payment from the Strait of Hormuz toll revenue has been deposited into the Central Bank of Iran's account. (Tasnim News Agency, Iran)

data.title: Murad's portfolio has recovered to $12 million, but is still down 82% from its peak.
data.description: Arkham reported that trader Murad's portfolio has recovered to approximately $12 million, up about 50% from its previous low of $7.8 million, but still down about 82% from its all-time high. Murad did not sell any SPX or other Meme tokens during this period.

data.title: South Korea Expands Debt Assessment to Include Virtual Assets
data.description: South Korea is set to enhance its debt assessment procedures by incorporating checks on debtors' financial and virtual asset holdings. According to NS3.AI, the Financial Services Commission announced that a revision to the Credit Information Use and Protection Act has been approved by the National Assembly. Previously, repayment evaluations primarily considered real estate and tax information. This amendment aims to address calls for more comprehensive asset evaluations to mitigate moral hazard.

data.title: OKX will launch OPGUSDT perpetual contracts.
data.description: OKX announced that it will officially launch the OPGUSDT perpetual contract on its website, app, and API at 17:00 (UTC+8) on April 23, 2026. According to available information, OpenGradient is a decentralized AI infrastructure used to host, execute, and validate models.

data.title: Euro Area Business Activity Contracts Amid Iran Conflict Impact
data.description: Business activity in the euro area has unexpectedly contracted for the first time since late 2024, driven by a significant decline in the services sector. Bloomberg posted on X that the ongoing conflict in Iran is exerting pressure on consumer sentiment, contributing to the downturn. The contraction marks a notable shift in the region's economic landscape, highlighting the broader implications of geopolitical tensions on market dynamics. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation as it unfolds, assessing potential impacts on future economic performance in the eurozone.

data.title: U.S. Awaits Iran's Response to Resume Peace Talks
data.description: The United States has announced that it is awaiting a response from Iran to move forward with peace negotiations between conflicting parties. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing diplomatic efforts to resolve tensions. The U.S. government has expressed its readiness to engage in discussions, contingent upon Iran's willingness to participate. This development comes amid heightened geopolitical tensions and efforts to stabilize the region. The U.S. aims to facilitate dialogue and foster a peaceful resolution, emphasizing the importance of cooperation from all involved parties.

data.title: Global Crypto Market Faces Volatility Amid Geopolitical and Policy Uncertainties
data.description: In April, the global cryptocurrency market experienced fluctuations influenced by geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, a leadership crisis at the Federal Reserve, and the listing of Morgan Stanley's Bitcoin ETF. According to PANews, Bitcoin demonstrated unique resilience during this period. However, the combination of geopolitical and policy uncertainties has made it challenging for the market to find a consensus on short-term direction.

data.title: Account Profits from Knicks vs. Hawks Event
data.description:
An account with over $790,000 in profits has made a significant move in the Knicks vs. Hawks event by purchasing a position worth approximately $52,700 on the Hawks winning. According to Odaily, the opening price was 49¢, and the current price stands at 48.5¢.

The series is currently tied at 1-1, with the Hawks having gained a home-court advantage after a comeback in Game 2. Despite the Knicks having a better regular-season record and a complete lineup, the fundamentals of both teams are closely matched, leading to a tight market expectation.

data.title: H100 has signed a binding strategic acquisition agreement, and its Bitcoin holdings are expected to increase to 3,500.
data.description: Swedish Bitcoin treasury company H100 announced that it has signed a binding strategic acquisition agreement. Upon completion of the transaction, the company's Bitcoin holdings are expected to increase to approximately 3,500 coins.

data.title: OpenAI reportedly poached several marketing executives from Coinbase.
data.description: Over the past year, several senior marketing executives from Coinbase have joined OpenAI, including former Chief Marketing Officer Kate Rouch, Head of Integrated Marketing Sarah Russell, and Head of International Marketing Elke Karstens. In addition, many former Coinbase employees have also moved to the AI ​​field, including policy, product, and data roles. Coinbase stated that with a team size exceeding 150 people, such personnel turnover is normal. (CoinDesk)

data.title: BlackRock Acquires $900 Million in Bitcoin in One Week
data.description: BlackRock has reportedly purchased approximately $900 million worth of Bitcoin within a single week. According to ChainCatcher, blockchain analytics platform Arkham monitored the transactions, revealing that BlackRock's ETF clients currently have an estimated average holding cost of $87,000 per Bitcoin.

data.title: Hyperliquid Platform Whale Positions Reach $3.575 Billion
data.description:
Hyperliquid platform whales currently hold positions totaling $3.575 billion, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions account for $1.849 billion, representing 51.72% of the total, while short positions amount to $1.726 billion, making up 48.28%. Long positions have seen a profit of $845,300, whereas short positions have incurred a loss of $26.0975 million.

Notably, a whale address, 0x0ddf..02, has taken a 3x full-margin short position on BTC at a price of $67,992.10, currently facing an unrealized loss of $10.1479 million.

data.title: Polymarket Traders Profit from Paris Weather Anomalies Amid Tampering Allegations
data.description: Two Polymarket accounts gained approximately $37,000 from Paris weather contracts following unusual temperature increases at Charles de Gaulle Airport Station on April 6 and 15. According to NS3.AI, BFMTV and Bubblemaps reported that the temperature readings temporarily rose above the surrounding conditions. Bubblemaps noted that one trader purchased NO shares on 18°C just before the temperature spike on April 15. Meteo France has reportedly lodged a police complaint concerning alleged tampering with its automated data systems.

data.title: Ethereum Faces Bearish RSI Divergence Amid Whale Accumulation
data.description: Ethereum has experienced a second bearish RSI divergence following an 8.88% correction last week. According to NS3.AI, this market movement coincides with an increase in whale holdings, which rose from 123.75 million ETH on April 19 to 123.91 million ETH by April 22.

data.title: India Refutes Claims of Crypto Payment for Strait of Hormuz Passage
data.description: India has dismissed reports suggesting that the tanker Sanmar Herald made payments in cash or cryptocurrency to Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, the Indian shipping ministry labeled the allegations as fake news. Sanmar Shipping, the company operating the tanker, also confirmed that no such payment incident took place. MARISKS had previously cautioned about scammers demanding Bitcoin or Tether for fraudulent transit clearances.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Former OpenAI Engineer Launches AI Hardware Company Blackstar Computers
data.description:
Former OpenAI Codex team engineer Daniel Edrisian has announced his departure from the company to establish Blackstar Computers, an AI hardware firm. According to Odaily, Blackstar Computers has successfully completed a $12 million seed funding round led by Abstract, with participation from SV Angel, Naval Ravikant, Chapter One, and Timeless.

Blackstar Computers aims to redefine computing devices by innovating across hardware, software, and interaction layers. Edrisian emphasized that while software development has reached maturity, enhancing human-AI interaction requires a fundamental overhaul at the operating system level. The company currently has a team of approximately eight members located in San Francisco and Shenzhen, and has yet to release any public products.

data.title: Uzbekistan Exempts Mining Income from Tax Until 2035 with New Initiative
data.description: Uzbekistan has introduced the Besqala Mining Valley in Karakalpakstan, offering a tax exemption on mining income until 2035. According to NS3.AI, the initiative allows residents to utilize renewable energy sources for cryptocurrency mining. Mined cryptocurrencies can be sold on both local and international exchanges, with the stipulation that sale proceeds are transferred to banks within Uzbekistan. This regime is exclusively available to legal entities that are registered and operating in Karakalpakstan.

data.title: Philippine Central Bank's Rate Hike Decision Not Unanimously Approved
data.description: The Philippine Central Bank recently announced that its decision to raise interest rates was not unanimously agreed upon by its board members. According to Jin10, the policy move reflects differing opinions among the bank's officials regarding the current economic conditions and the appropriate monetary response. The central bank's decision comes amid ongoing discussions about inflationary pressures and economic growth in the region. The lack of consensus highlights the complexity of balancing economic stability with growth objectives.

data.title: France's April Composite PMI Falls Short of Expectations
data.description: France's composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) for April recorded a preliminary value of 47.6, according to Jin10. This figure is below the anticipated 48.6 and the previous month's reading of 48.8. The PMI is a key indicator of economic health, with values below 50 suggesting contraction in the sector.

data.title: Blackstar, founded by a former OpenAI engineer, has raised $12 million in seed funding.
data.description: Former OpenAI Codex engineer Daniel Edrisian has announced his departure to found AI hardware company Blackstar Computers. The company has completed a $12 million seed funding round, led by Abstract, with participation from SV Angel, Naval Ravikant, Chapter One, and Timeless. Blackstar positions itself as a new type of computing device, planning to reshape the computing experience from hardware, software, and interaction perspectives. Edrisian stated that while software development is now mature, further improvements in human-AI interaction require innovation at the operating system level. The company currently has approximately eight employees, located in San Francisco and Shenzhen, and has not yet launched any publicly available products.
